rent.note: furnished 1BR in districts V, VI, VII nomad area; €670–€900

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; heating significant in winter

diningOut.note: ruin bars and restaurants; good value

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; growing scene in central districts

misc.note: thermal baths, gym, toiletries, entertainment, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Peak tourist months get higher multipliers.

evidence.claim: 1BR central districts €670–€900/mo; rents rising due to remote worker influx
evidence.quote: the cost of living in Budapest for an expat in 2026 averages €670–€900 per month for rent in central districts (V, VI, VII)
Groceries cost €200–€350 per month, and a monthly public transport pass for unlimited travel costs approximately €22

evidence.claim: comfortable nomad budget €1,200–€2,000/mo depending on lifestyle
evidence.quote: you can live in Budapest comfortably on a budget of about €1,200-€2,000 a month, depending on your lifestyle

evidence.claim: outer districts rent €380–€550; rents pushed up by foreign remote workers
evidence.quote: In outer districts (XIII and beyond), rent ranges from €380–€550
